g.text: Core depends only on ports (interfaces). Adapters implement ports and are injected via the composition root (main.py).
Pure business logic — no I/O, no frameworks
— UUID, status, statusInfo snapshots, remote tracking
Fetch, cache & transform remote processes via handler pipeline
(enforce ID → fill defaults → sanitize metadata → rewrite links)
Create local jobs, forward execution, derive status, schedule polling, notify observers
core/interfaces/ — abstract contracts the core depends on
Implement ports — injected into core · adapters/
Not yet implemented — will fulfill existing or new ports
Outermost layer: wires all adapters, creates factories, starts Uvicorn
1. Instantiate adapters (ProviderConfig, AioHttp, ColonProcessId, InMemoryRepo, SiteInfo, Logging)
2. Build factories: process_manager_factory, job_manager_factory → passed to create_app()
3. FastAPI lifespan wires managers, attaches observers (StatusHistory, Polling, ResultsVerification)
Core depends on abstract ports; adapters are swappable
StatusDerivation strategies evaluated in priority order
Constructor injection; factories from composition root
— Business rules, domain models, ports. No I/O.
— Receives external requests, calls into core.
— Implements a port; core calls it for infra.
A piece of code is an adapter (not core) if it:
• Could be swapped for an alternative without
If none of the above apply → it belongs in core.
